item.title: The US Treasury is naked shorting oil futures — 39 replies — 156.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
The US Treasury is naked shorting oil futures

Open thread • Replies: 39 • Updated: 2026-03-16 23:53 UTC

OP

I’ve been watching this shit all day and it’s wild af. Oil futures get hyper dumped in giant chunks, which causes the price of spot to react, like the retarded dog on a leash it is. The thing is - everything is getting worse; tankers aren’t sailing, oil reserves are getting more dire, and there is no resolution in sight.

Meanwhile someone is hyper dumping oil futures like their job depends on it. Because it does. The fucking US Treasury is desperately trying to keep oil prices in check by gaming the market, but all they’re really doing is destroying the legitimacy of the futures market entirely. This little game works a few times until the larger market realizes what’s going on and literally just abandons the futures market for spot product.

Unfucking believable how corrupt and stupid this administration is.

Early replies (first 3)

61992408
Maybe the retardmaxers were right about needing to shut off our brains to make money

61992411
Mossad is also trying to FUD oil all over social media.

61992416
>>61992372
Next time, don't get your investing thesises from seething third worlders and jam boys.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61992531
I'm still unable to understand how it works. Like the simple concept of futures I get it. Like we agree to buy or sell something at a specific time for a specific price. Like for insurance and shit. But from here it gets all so complicated. How can that affect the current price instantly?

61992564
>>61992508
Manipulation makes the inevitable correction worse. Oil is just the latest to be gamed at this level. Futures are or rather were part of the supply demand mechanism for markets used by both consumers and producers. When this gets out of whack you get supply disruptions, in the case of existing distruptions these get worse and last longer. Doing this with baseline commodities is beyond retarded but you'll come to understand that soon. Look at how beef prices were manipulated and what that did to producers, once it got paired with actual shortages due to disease the farmers who got fucked repeatedly sold the entirety of their herds rather than breed them, profit windows became artificially short thus no one wanted to play the rigged game any more. This will end in tears but atleast they still can still maintain fiction for the day.

61992582
>>61992531
It doesn’t. The lines on the chart and the market aren’t the same thing. If you have access to enough leverage then the price can theoretically be whatever you want it to be.

61992606
>>61992442
The main appeal of capitalism is efficiency, part of this is reliance on real data for moves. IE there is a squeeze on 20% of oil supply and long term destruction of infrastructure, futures should pump thus encouraging everyone outside of affected region to produce maximum possible oil mitigating the shortage as much as humanly possible, if futures stay elevated this extends to exploration and affiliate fields and you get expansion of that 80% long term and the market becomes more resilient for this event in the future. Artificially lower the prices however and the market doesn't react this way. Even if/when futures are sidelined you have nothing to show for the period in which the market was reacting inorganically. And the benefits of the futures market are lost.

61992607
>>61992529
>Oil
>Scarce
>There's roughly 2billion barrels of oil in reserve from G7 alone
>There's somewhere around 2trillion barrels left unmined.
How do I explain to you that you have no idea what you're talking about?

You're literally betting that one little scuffle, not even a fucking war, with some retarded inbred sand people who fuck children is going to end the world economy??? Come on. Take your deluluglasses off.
>>61992564
There is no manipulation. Oil is an extremely liquid market, with insane supply. The market pumped on speculation, nothing more. There was no oil crisis.

61992717
>>61992607
Can I get a job fed posting? Does it pay well? I hope they pay you enough to compensate for your dignity for acting like suddenly cutting off 20% of the world’s oil supply is a nothing burger. I mean; Australia will soon start rationing fuel, but it’s no big deal - right? Fucking retard

61992727
>>61992372

61992732
Something something Comex something something silver something something 2 weeks

61992911
>>61992607
>There is no manipulation
Glowmigger detected

61992954
>>61992607
>retarded inbred sand people who fuck children
Thats no way to speak about your president. Seek Christ!

61992995
>>61992607
>roughly 2billion barrels of oil in reserve from G7
400 million while the total consumption is 100 million a day. Oh and on top of that gulf countries have nowhere to store the oil and are reducing their production as well. Meaning they began to conserve their wells which are very expensive to untap again and which takes months to do.

You dumb glow fag

61993065
>>61992508
Listen to people smarter than you, and there are even some in this very thread that told you as much

61993089
>>61992372
Midterms are all that matter, nigger. Life is about daytrading someone else's future

61993104
>>61992372
Also, if this is what it takes to break all foreign trust and dependance on the usa, so be it.

61993188
>>61992372
This is 100% happening. Probably using HFT cutouts like Jane Street. "Capitalism" is fake and gay. We need real capitalism.

61993233
>>61992372
Now you understand what has been happening in gold and silver markets for decades.

61993270
>>61993233
If a market can be successfully 'manipulated' for decades, doesn't the 'manipulation' simply become the actual market? What's the functional difference? Real manipulation should be impossible over longer periods of time due to market self-correction mechanisms.

61993299
>>61993270
Naked shorting is only a problem if someone tries to take delivery of the underlying physical good. For a long time buyers were happy settling for cash and not taking delivery. Recent price moves in precious metals are because of demand for physical. That is the self-correcting feature and its playing out slowly.

61993347
>>61992372
They have no choice but to do this. They need the petrodollar to survive, which requires the military operations in Iran to succeed. If the US government cannot achieve Iranian regime change before it becomes politically/economically impossible to continue the war, then we will be forever changed as a nation. Retreating from Iran almost certainly means the collapse of US dominance in the region, and thus the collapse of the petrodollar.
Yes the admin is corrupt and stupid, but they also have no choice. This is really the calm before the storm my friend, if the petrodollar collapses then dollar dominance will follow, and then the US will actually need to answer for its national debt. We're in for some rough times ahead.

61993408
>>61992717
>suddenly cutting off 20% of the world’s oil supply
Literally wrong. There's ~2 trillion barrels of oil in the supply. 20M barrels is not 20%. It's 20% of the oil produced per day. Which is a literal fucking nothing in the supply.
It's 20% of 5% of the supply
Which is 1% of the supply.
Literal.
Fucking.
Nothing.
But that oil is still being produced, there's still ~95m barrels being produced daily. And the countries that would be buying that oil still have plenty of reserves.
>Can I get a job fed posting?
No, sand people are barred.
>>61992995
The Iranians didn't destroy 20million barrels worth of production. The production remains largely unchanged.
What was affected, however, was the ability to get that production onto the market through the strait of homos.

An extremely small percentage of oil production was lost. Maybe like 3%.
>Oh and on top of that gulf countries have nowhere to store the oil and are reducing their production as well.
Lmao don't you see how this contradicts your point?
>We have so much oil we can't store it!
>BUT THERE'S A HUGE SUPPLY RISK!!!

There is no supply shock.

61993426
>>61992372
They’ve been doing the same thing to silver for the last 20 years, and getting caught doing so and fined btw

61993437
>>61993408
oh ok thats why a ton of countries will be running out in 10 days

61993444
>>61992607
you're a fucking retard lmao

61993497
>>61993437
You're full of shit. Most countries still have around a year of reserves. And their imports of oil have not been cut to zero.
It's just that oil leaving the middle east is being held hostage by the literal terrorists.

But there's still plenty of oil around the world that can be exported to those countries. Not only that, the strait is simply the cheapest way to get the oil out, there are other options. Just with less throughput.
>>61993444
Prove me wrong.
Oh wait, you literally cannot.

61993514
>>61992372
>naked shorting oil
While you are right you are also wrong, it is not naked, they just steal the oil if they are getting called.

61993520
>>61992372
people like you spreading this idea are so beyond retarded. the US is the largest oil producers in the world now and the SPR is releasing 172M barrels of oil. the US Treasury could short futures and fucking deliver the oil to the market this way. its not a naked short you fucking moron THEY HAVE THE OIL TO DELIVER.

61993675
>>61993408
they can't store it because they have no storage facilities. Where they are supposed to put all that oil that they can't put on tankers?

61993780
We've been in an economic recession for months now. Like covid and 2008 levels. Oil is about to plummet sub $50

61993797
Dont look up onion futures

61993807
>>61992416
Such a forced glownigger meme
Why can't glowniggers say shitskin even on pol? Because they are shitskins?

item.title: ARISE, wagecucks, you subjects of capitalism, for yet another week of servitude awaits. — 14 replies — 53.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
ARISE, wagecucks, you subjects of capitalism, for yet another week of servitude awaits.

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-03-16 23:42 UTC

OP

ARISE, wagecucks, you subjects of capitalism, for yet another week of servitude awaits. obey the commands of your rat-faced masters -- as if they were from the mouth of god himself!

Early replies (first 3)

61990347
>>61990340
i wish i had a job. Coming up 1 year on unemployed soon.

61990351
>>61990347

61990421
>>61990347
What did you do before and why haven't you found anything since?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61990465
>>61990340
looking forward to my fat paycheck this week.

61990482
>>61990340
I fucking hate capitalism,
>forces people to work jobs they don't enjoy
>women do stupid jobs that require 0 effort and that's okay
>niggers live on gibs without working and live on workers tax money
>tax money used in stupid shit like giving money to israel
>elites don't work at all and live better than those who work 1000 times
slavery is better than capitalism

61990539
I’m a professor on spring break jokes on you. Summer is right around the corner too

61990555
>>61990340
>god himself
god is not a man

61990837
>>61990340
ARISE neets for yet another week of gooning and tendies

61990971
>>61990555
God can't be a roastie, so he is a he. Simple.

61991029
>>61990539

61991547
>>61991029
Literature, theory and film. It’s comfy.

61992562
>>61990340
You know that working allows you to buy things with the money you make right?

61993234
>>61990837
Kek, i love this pasta

61993774

item.title: cope edition >Latest developments on the US strikes against Iran >Some helpful links to numb the pain >European indices… — 352 replies — 360.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
cope edition >Latest developments on the US strikes against Iran >Some helpful links to numb the pain >European indices…

Open thread • Replies: 352 • Updated: 2026-03-16 22:59 UTC

OP

cope edition

>Latest developments on the US strikes against Iran
https://iran.liveuamap.com/
https://x.com/sentdefender

>Some helpful links to numb the pain
https://findahelpline.com/
https://www.nvidia.com/refunds/
https://www.whitehouse.gov/refunds
https://jlingz.com/

>European indices
https://live.euronext.com/en

>Educational sites:
https://www.investopedia.com
https://www.khanacademy.org/economics-finance-domain

>Financial TV Streams:
https://www.newslive.com/american/cnbc.html
https://www.livestreamy.net/bloomberg/

>Charts:
https://www.tradingview.com
https://www.finscreener.com
https://www.portfoliovisualizer.com

>Screeners: so you feel like in the movies
https://finviz.com/
https://etfdb.com/

>Gambling:
https://www.optionsplaybook.com/options-introduction/
https://www.optionsprofitcalculator.com
https://optionstrat.com/
https://www.optionistics.com/quotes/option-prices

>Pre-Market and Live data:
https://www.investing.com/indices/indices-futures
https://finance.yahoo.com/

>Calendars
https://www.marketwatch.com/economy-politics/calendar
https://www.earningswhispers.com/calendar
https://www.cmegroup.com/trading/interest-rates/countdown-to-fomc.html
https://www.mortgagenewsdaily.com/data/building-permits

>Boomer Investing 101: so zoomers with meme stocks can outperform you
https://www.bogleheads.org/wiki/Getting_started
https://www.sec.gov/search-filings

>Misc smg:
https://www.financialjuice.com/home
https://finance.yahoo.com/trending-tickers
https://market24hclock.com/
https://www.dividendchannel.com/drip-returns-calculator
https://brokerchooser.com/
https://www.chathamfinancial.com/technology/us-market-rates

previously on /smg/
>>61991543

Early replies (first 3)

61992012
You forgot WHAT?!

61992015
Strip for me

61992017
TRUMP'S MINION BESSENT IS MANIPULATING THE MARKET. THERE IS A GAP BETWEEN FUTURES AND REALITY. AND REALITY ALWAYS WINS.

SCUM!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61992652
>>61992651
kek at you

61992654
why are we up? what changed?

61992656
>>61992647
If you don’t want this house of cards to collapse you’re unironically evil.

61992659
They're bombing McDonald's again.

61992660
>>61992438
did he really say that?

61992661
the 4 figure oil baggies are starting to lash out

61992662
>>61992647
I don't fantasize. Bad shit happens all the time.

61992663
>>61992639
I'm still down kek need to be back over 50. But it doesn't matter, this will be worth hundreds soon enough.

61992664
>>61992654
Tuesday dumparacha in play

61992665
>>61992651
>>61992652
Now kiss and make up, jeets
We're all good

61992669
>hurr theres ww3 happening but ill long stocks and short oil

61992670
>>61992148
Ex chad here, 3.5 years for me. I have a body count in the high 20s, so my ego doesn't really have anything to prove. My long term gf dumping me kinda just melted my brain. Idk what happened, but I have brutal social anxiety after that. Getting dumped probably made my brain turn off all the serotonin and dopamine levers as some maladaptive survival mechanism

61992672
>>61992654
I bought

61992673
war is bullish, oil shocks are bullish

61992674
Remember when Oil went negative for a couple of days? That was fun.

https://youtu.be/LUpV2LSXn80?si=xqwrSdmRQglFfgh6

61992678
>>61992663
relax, its one of the few comapnies who is solving the energy question with the data center infra, we're gucci, just hold and average down if you can

61992679
>>61992676
>>61992676
>>61992676

61992680
>>61992669
There is?

61992681
>>61992669
It's literally shills trying to contain the damage.

61992684
>>61992669
>WWIII is happening
>Look outside
>Suns out, birds are chirping, the sky is blue and the grass is green
>Check monitor
>OIL dumping
Lmao.

61992696
markets are getting extremely sharty, low liquidity, flailing market throwing a tantum that it has to go down

61992720
>President Trump is mulling strikes against key Iranian oil infrastructure on Kharg Island, according to US Ambassador to the UN Mike Waltz

61992724
>>61992670
My body count is over 50+ and I gave up on women about 2020.
Been a wanker since.

61992726
>>61992660
Yes

61992871
>>61992678
I literally said it will be 100s lol

61992877
Oil sisters... its over...
We will be oil baggies forever...
$92 and falling...

61993086
Im officially an oil bagholder. Please laugh at me

61993581
>>61992048
Hope you set a stop loss lol

61993590
>>61992049
Just hold you fucking moron. Have some fucking patience, geez!

61993596
>>61992053
A covid-lite 2.0 economy that will give me another chance to make fuck loads of money from a small investment in oil.

None of those other things really matter on a direct personal level if I make fuckloads of money. Oil dropping to the floor is the entry point I've been waiting for.

item.title: The next 100x — 29 replies — 116.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
The next 100x

Open thread • Replies: 29 • Updated: 2026-03-16 23:55 UTC

OP

Sealed PSA 9 Charizard packs.
Your welcome b. Stay poor

Early replies (first 3)

61991676
>>61991664
What's the fucking point of sealing that pack?? Why would anyone buy it and not open it?? Are you guys stupid?

61991690
>>61991676
The packs are a collectable on their own. Retards with your mindset is what makes it valuable

61991751
Entire PSA-9 market cap of 1999 Base Set sealed booster packs is basically a microcap roughly 600 PSA-9 packs exist and at about $1.5k each that’s ~$900k total market cap

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61991909
>>61991751
Congrats on your extremely low liquidity investment choice.

61991959
>>61991909
>low liquidity
Buying a sealed Base Set Charizard pack today is roughly equivalent to buying:

Bitcoin at about £300


>The price only goes up as people get priced out, fomo, and jake pauler idiot rich kids opening up for the bragging rights to say how rich they are making more go up. This is an easy x100 over the next 2-3 years

61991977
>>61991676
>opening a pack that has a 99% chance of losing you money
If you pay $500 for a pack and most of the cards are worth 50 cents, with an extremely small chance of pulling a $450 card, what sense would that make to open it instead of holding onto it sealed?

61991981
>>61991664
This shit is only valuable as long as people have interest in it. Like stamps, or commemorative coins. You will lose money.

61991986
>>61991690
>>61991977
What value is buying the pack?? Isn't it the chance that you could get a sweet card? IF YOUR NOT OPENING THE PACK WHY WOULD YOU BUY IT ???? HELLO?

61992371
>>61991986
This is why jews run the world

61992657
>>61991909
I sold some pokemon cards recently and let me tell you they are NOT low liquidity and even with ebays disgustingly high fees they're very profitable.

61992691
>>61991676
the seal is for authenticity. it's basically saying this is a real pack feel free to crack the seal and ensure the cards are legit. for packs, the psa seal is meant to be cracked. not sure why they grade it though that's kinda autistic.

61992695
>>61991751
Unlimited is trash.
PSA 9 is trash
If you didn’t buy 1st Ed PSA 10 packs at $400 or lower each, it’s over for you.

61992701
>>61991664
based poke investor

61992719
Why do these threads not get deleted immediately? Pokemon cards are not an investment

61992741
>>61991664
Sealed packs are actually a retarddd investment

61992754
>>61992719
we are making money. linkies are not.

61992787
>>61992719
>Pokemon cards are not an investment
They're beating all my investments

61992882
sorry my english is bad but basically pokemon is one giant ponzi scheme like other collectable bubbles it works like this
>((((auction houses)))) and (((((influencers))))) see a hobby or interest that is easy to corner the market with, a rare game or card or whatever BS, either buy up the supply and then once they control the supply, just keep selling it back and forth to themselves or other people in their circle at increasingly higher prices, also see (((private sales))) on instagram or forums where people can just announce that they have sold card at a certain price without any proofs
>now every retard who had 0 interest in these cards despite them being sat for a decade on ebay shops for 1/100th of their current prices are now all piling in, and thus the "pokeinvestor" is born
>also add ridiculous premiums for ((((grading)))), the graders then charge not for just their grading service but you get charged based on the final value of the card, so now all these cards are at retarded hyperinflated prices, it's basically a licence to print money because they charge on the cards perceived worth
>oh also i forgot to add, these large collectible auction houses are run by people who have been fined in the past for manipulating coin markets in the 80s
>oh i forgot to mention that in auction houses TOS they reserve the right to bid on their own item. this means anyone else bidding on the items against the auction houses is instantly at a 20-30% disadvantage because the auction house doesnt have to pay the buyers fee
>add to that all of the other bullshit companies surrounding it and their (((ceos)))), even bullshit like whatnot now has a RICO lol.....

the rabbit hole goes so deep, there's just a whole web of companies involved and this is why now any hobby that was once enjoyable like retro gaming or trading cards is now just a bullshit scam with retards telling you it's an investment

61992898
>>61992882
>the graders then charge not for just their grading service but you get charged based on the final value of the card,
thats only a PSA thing, not every grading company is greedy. yes the big sales are astroturfed but they cant control the market for cards with a massive population

61992984
>>61991751
The market cap of my shit is literally $0. Invest today

61993076
>>61991986
Its a collectable.
I am 99% certain you are a nigger that does not grasp the concept of scarcity and collectibles.

61993140
>>61992741
>Sealed packs are actually a retarddd investment
basically up only for 30 years anon, beats s&p and even nvidia

61993191
>>61993140
you forget the 2003-2019 era where it was the gayest bull shit imaginable

61993439
>>61991981
I dont deal with cards. Pokemon is one of the most succesful IPs of all time though. Even more successful than Star Wars. Its going to last alot longer than you think.

61993586
>>61993191
whilst demand was rock bottom for a long time (maybe till 2016) base set packs definitely cost alot more in 2016 than 2003 so my point still stands

61993629
>>61993439
Agreed, despite everything after gen 4 being a sharp downturn (aside from pokemon go which was instead a severe waste of potential)
But paraphernalia like this only matters to pure nostalgia nerds that were hitting their formative years in the 90s-2000s
Not only are they now hitting middle age but they don't have anywhere near the spare wealth the generations before them had

61993710
>>61993586
its in a mellenial bubble and will phase out of interest. every game ever since red/blue has been red/blue remastered. until they make another good game its not going to generate the same kind of hype ever again. people will get old and sell these cards to pay for bills, except the zoomers of 2030, 2040 ect wont give a fuck about pokemon. unless they make a good ass game

61993733
Bought these two bad boys last week :)

61993815
>>61993710
pretty sure every pokemon game has outsold the previous (mainline) dont fall for the fud, pokemon aint going anywhere its the new disney

item.title: The GIGACHAD just did it again. 22,337 BTC added to his stack. He is getting closer to 1 million btc — 22 replies — 88.0/hr

Open thread • Replies: 22 • Updated: 2026-03-16 23:56 UTC

OP

The GIGACHAD just did it again. 22,337 BTC added to his stack.

He is getting closer to 1 million btc

Early replies (first 3)

61990513
>>61990491

61990575
>>61990491
what a madman, pamp it

61990576
He might become one of the most powerful GOYIM in the world.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61990588
>>61990491
This is not going to end well.

61990633
Who’s going to buy it if he’s buying it all and owns it all?

61990690
>>61990633
He can't own it all because my coins are not for sale.

61990817
>>61990576
The Jews will be forced to bow to Saylor. Truly kino

61990842
all dose satoshis

61990985
>>61990633
he never sell

61990998
And it can all be lost by making one typo in the blockchain address.

61991053
>He is getting closer

Satoshi still holds way more, which he mined for himself almost for free!!!!!!

61992027
>>61990633
He's creating all kinds of products using his btc. The STRC thing is getting huge and at this pace, he will just keep buying

61992128
>>61990633
he will rent it out. you can lease a coin from him at (((fair rates)))

61992161
>>61990491
Does anyone know about the safeguards and plans he installed in case he doesn't wake up one day?

61992164
>>61990633
Greater Eggman theory

61992259
>>61990491
>*buys the local top in yo'ure path*
But yeah, BTC never goes below the ATL before the previous ATH. His strategy just works on the long run.
>>61992161
Do you think he has cold wallets under his bed and he just knows the phrases by heart? The coins are owned by his company, but by him directly.

61993238
>>61992164
he's got the master plan

61993248
His whole buying strategy seems to be to put in a big market order causing the price to spike 3% then hope retail fomo’s in behind him. Hasn’t worked yet.

61993309
>>61990633
He will sell all at market

61993720
>>61990633
>2026
>retards still don’t know how bitcoin works

61993738
>>61993720
Him plus literally 90% of humanity.. And then tell me why we can't just kill them all?

61993818
>>61990491
literally the egg man

61993820
This motherfucker is insane, I wish I knew what was going on in his head.

item.title: LINK is starting its hyper super cycle once in a lifetime run. — 43 replies — 61.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
LINK is starting its hyper super cycle once in a lifetime run.

Open thread • Replies: 43 • Updated: 2026-03-16 23:16 UTC

OP

LINK is starting its hyper super cycle once in a lifetime run. Hope your bags are packed. This is unironically the moment we've been waiting for.

Early replies (first 3)

61990544
Are you saying artemis wasn't a larp

61990577
>>61990540
It’s gonna dump soon. We have been doing this dance for almost a decade now. Sadly there is not much to expect until we have actual real life adoption.

61990580
>>61990540
Its 5% bro, wake me when we pass $30

Insider trading on the clarity act is unlikely

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61991747
>>61990544
>calls himself artemis
>surely he wasn't larping us

61991947
>>61990544
Shartemis lied

61991990
Finally I’ve been waiting since… gush… 1945 I reckon… Now get those jap bastards fellohuh uh MARINES.. yes that’s it. God bless

61992009
>>61990540
>tfw I saw on Twitter that some early LINKer just passed away

It's weird to think that a lot of us have been invested in this stuff for so long, dreaming about making it, meanwhile life is moving on and LINK marines are passing away. I don't like to think about it, but it hurts.

61992087
>>61990810
Call when PEPE is 1 penny

61992274
>>61990544
99% of namefags are larps, but artemis wasn't.

61992452
insiders will buy before the clarity act passes

61992474
>>61990540
My bags have been packed for so long that I’ve missed many other trips into outer space

61992480
>>61990540
Its finally time bros

61993046
>>61990544
I was not larping. But your skeptical timing is perfect. Let’s unironically chat in a two weeks, I can’t wait to hear what you think then.

61993125
>>61993046
>>61992274

I've kept the faith. You have appeared several times over in unwanted premonitions. This is how I know, and the seethe will be glorious. I've been correct with every single prediction since last summer. I will be right again. Artemis warned you all.

>t. 6th density neet

61993133
>>61993046
Aha! The faker once again! Begone!
Hark! Tis I! The true Artemis!

61993139
IT'S HAPPENING!
TONIGHT!
>t. Artemis

61993181
>>61990540
link is being dragged up by the entire crypto market. It isn't even up as much as shiba inu or doge in the last week.

61993184
>>61993181
Peel

61993255
>>61993184
Yes, that is another crypto project that like doge and shiba inu is up more than link.

61993261
>>61993255
Self-checked.

61993307
>>61993261
i just completed the captcha to tell you that you're a faggot. but you already know that

61993317
Is any of those fuckers the real one I don’t feel like back checking but I’m also contempt and feel like Link is about to rip like a mother fucker

61993321
>>61990540
I sold 40 link yesterday to buy a nice barbell and I'm glad I did. My body feels good even as link is about to dump to oblivion yet again.

61993402
>>61993046
fuck, I don't have enough link

61993441
>>61993317
yea, the one that ends in AjQ

61993457
>>61993441
LIES! BEGONE, HERETIC!

61993499
>>61993457
AHHHH A GAY GUY

61993511
>Got 10k linkies in my early twenties
>Boughted since then and am now early 30s and have 100k link
It's been so fucking long, really hope we will be awarded in the end.

61993517
>>61993046
the only one that ever mattered was 42. and he is no where to be found since 2021...

61993549
>>61993511
Unironically I hope so, anon

61993565
>>61993511
anon you'll make it when link tops at $70 well played

61993585
>>61993307
Very reddit reply

61993655
>>61993046
I want to believe
Never selling

item.title: Ripple cucking XRP holders again — 68 replies — 47.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Ripple cucking XRP holders again

Open thread • Replies: 68 • Updated: 2026-03-16 22:32 UTC

OP

Oof. Thanks for the money doe

Early replies (first 3)

61970723
>>61970713
>xerp
>creep toes
pick one!

61971052
>>61970713
89383 xrp left

61971076
>>61970713
stick to your containment

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61978659
>>61974686
You wanna know something really funny?
Every time Ripple wants to sell XRP to institutions, they would have to admit they're selling securities kek
See pic, it's the form they'd have to fill out.

Maybe that's why they haven't done it at all, and have been selling XRP exclusively to retail ever since the lawsuit and waiver.

61978914
how does the theory of all the XRP escrow is already alloted out to companies make sense if they sell like 1b XRP every month from there?

I know that most of it gets relocked, but assuming the 300m per month is avg, that means they sell 3.6b XRP per year. How long have they been selling that per year? How much was sold or locked away in 2018/2019/2020 etc?
Would that be sustainable if all of the escrow was already allotted to people?

Whats the maximum ratio of actual sales escrow to supposed locked/alloted escrow allowed to make this theory make sense?

61978921
>>61970713
Has the company/coin ever in its history done anything productive for society?

61979005
>Chainshitters absolutely seething over xrp
Yup, I'm thinking we're gonna wagmi really hard really soon

61979017
>>61979005
the thread was about Ripple, and this freak started manically fudding Link out of nowhere >>61972246

61979223
>>61979017
>out of nowhere
Kek, you'd had to have been living under a rock to not be aware that the majority of xrp seethe comes from mindbroken linkbaggies

61979295
>>61972246
all lies.
Ripple built their business around ledger that can be sold fitted to customer's needs.
XRP is just marketing product and IF someone would use XRPL it would use RLUSD or other stablecoin as gas.

61982361
>>61979295
RLUSD can't be used as gas, XRP is the only counterparty-less asset on ledger that will or can ever exist and is the most efficient and default used for auto-bridging between them, particularly between differently denominated assets, like bonds or stocks or tokenized moneymarkets or MPT tokens or stable dollars or stable yen or stable...etc. Lol You act like the stablecoin was a wrench in our thesis when OUR ENTIRE THESIS was LITERALLY ALWAYS that there would be hundreds of stablecoins and walled gardens and traditional and blockchain ledgers, which is literally the point. Ripple have acquired a broker dealer that processes trillions of dollars annually and is going to use XRPL for post-trade settlement. They are literally building an end-to-end institutional tech stack that uses XRP at every point. Their entire corporate action is basically building a business that brings XRP to corporate finance and function as broadly and deeply focused on liquidity base layers as possible and they have done an extremely good job doing so:
https://ripple.com/insights/institutional-defi-on-xrpl-scaling-real-world-finance-with-xrp-at-the-core/

Even if it makes you mad, this is inarguably the case.

61982372
>>61979017
You are either new or disingenuous. The same 2 or 3 retards make these threads to cope and get BTFO every single time. This was not a new and random mention and has years of precedent. They NEVER know how to defend their attacks or even their own technology, they literally just throw shit into the fan and get shocked and mad and very flustered and confused when it blows back in their face.

61982379
>>61978659
This literally isn't true. Any secondary programmatic sales (which is how they do almost all of their sales) are implicitly ruled to not be securities offerings and XRP itself is the LITERAL ONLY other crypto to be declared a non-security officially except for BTC, regardless of what future admin decides. XRP is not a security, certain sales offerings are. Oranges are not securities, certain structured offerings of orange groves can be.

61983504
>>61982379
>Any secondary programmatic sales are implicitly ruled to not be securities offerings
Yes, sales to retail via exchanges (aka programmatic sales) are not securities.

>which is how they do almost all of their sales
Exactly right, all of the XRP sold by Ripple is sold to retail.

This is exactly what I've been saying all along.
Glad you finally saw the light lmao

61983508
>>61982372
>Ripple triple dips by selling both tokens and stock, and then using the token sales to buy back the stock like the scammers they are
>"REEEEE FUCKING LINKIES AAAAA"

lmao

61985136
>>61983504
XRP is itself not a security and they literally haven't sold any to retail, they sell directly with buffer to institutional clients in a demand neutral way. Any crypto offering marketed in a particular way is a security offering. XRP is actually open to more forms of offering with clarity as a non-security because it is federally recognized as not being one (only other coin is bitcoin). Ripple can structure the sale to not be a securities offering VERY easily and have been doinig so throughout the suit over the last several years, but do not sell to retail and account for <1% of total volume which affects price less than a cent literally. Chainlink Labs dump directly on your head directly from node rewards anytime there is a 5% pump.
>>61983508
This is really deep cope. Lol You chose wrong-you are getting a whole nother attempt at choosing and buying with the dump that is happening/coming. Making it is more meaningful than signaling obsessive cult adherence to a fucking crypto project that was LITERALLY built to randomly insert rent seeking fees and solve ZERO problems and improve nothing of Chainlink Labs' products, who subsidize use and largely are used just for price feeds. You don't get anything by entombing yourself permanently in a corny culture of late normie greed fags hoping lightning strikes twice because you are incapable of forecasting trend. All you get is pissed off and carved out to being a really weird unlovable type of NPC. Choose to make it instead.

61985603
>>61985136
>they literally haven't sold any to retail
You just said yourself selling to retail ("programmatic sales") is how they do almost all of their sales.

>they sell directly
They can't. Not without filing a "notice of exempt offering of securities", which they have never done for any sale of XRP.
Probably because filing this document means admitting they're selling securities lol

61985792
>>61972246
>building an end to end tech stack that can establish XRP as the liquidity base of defi and the evolution of tradfi and the bridge between them
this has not been built
you're delusional

61985918
>>61970713
But mcap is 85 billion so they will just instant sell anything they buy for profit

61986807
>>61985792
>t-this has not been built
>y-you're delusional

61987872
>>61985603
Literal retard. They sell programmatically through exchange through blind bid auctions in a DEMAND NEUTRAL way arranging exact >>61985792
It literally has. Hidden Road (now Ripple Prime) is a prime broker that handles back end for trillions in settlement annually with special emphasis in digital assets and privileged positioning for its build. GTreasury handles liquidity optimization and treasury management for thousands of companies and many Fortune 500s (bringing their idle capital onchain and moving through it), Metaco/Standard Custody for institutional grade custody used by many of the world's biggest banks already. They have a national bank charter with the OCC. Rail adds virtual accounts for collections and automated fiat-stablecoin conversion/settlement. Palisade gives wallet-as-a-service custody with MPC security, fast provisioning, multi-chain, and treasury automation for scalable fund sweeping. Their platform unifies collection, custody, exchange, settlement, and payout across fiat and digital assets in a single seamless API driven solution. Literal end-to-end solution doing exactly what I said and built piecewise with XRP at the heart of every single piece of it. Their corporate strategy is literally to drive XRP into the deep system of things by building the corporate architecture around it and having others enter it through them. Complete opposite to Chainlink who just shill to retards by publishing pictures of numbers that never translate to use which is only used as a jewish fee insertion for no reason. Pic related.

61987881
>>61985603
Literal retard. They sell programmatically on exchange through blind auctions in a DEMAND NEUTRAL way arranging exact same amount purchases for institutional clients. Their net impact on the exchange is 0. They literally do this already and have throughout the lawsuit while they had the injunction limiting their sales. You are SO a disingenuous, low IQ cope clinging and desperate.

61987900
>>61970713
should I sell my chainlink?
I'm up ~3x still on dca but chainlinkfag keeps losing his mind over xrp and I'm thinking it must be over for link

61987906
>>61987872
>>61987881
Yes, Ripple has been selling XRP exclusively through blind sales via exchanges ("programmatic sales"). In other words: to retail.

This is the third time you're agreeing with me on the same exact thing.
Are you sure you're ok?

61989506
>>61987906
No. Lol This is literally not remotely hard to understand. Ripple set up equivalent bids/asks between themselves and customers and sell through exchange to them in exact proportion having ZERO impact on demand. For like 9 months this was the fud you spammed and know it "it doesn't increase demand!!!" You are pretending to be a low IQ faggot but you are literally just hopeless without a leg to even beg to stand on in your own thesis or your own fud and are desperate to hope your gay rhetoric will obscure what is easily demonstrable reality. I cannot even imagine being like you. And the fact this is the best you can come up with, collectively as a group, is why you have failed so totally in messaging and convincing people (because your project is a literal founder dump scam) and is a huge vote in direction of XRP as good and link as irredeemable. Link has nothing to do with CLL services and is just an introduction of friction into its process to collect rent. It solves and does nothing. Become a real person again.

61989766
>>61970713
>people familiar with the matter
>under anonymity
Oh so they made it up.

61990596
>>61987872
HAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHA

>>61989506
>Ripple set up equivalent bids/asks between themselves and customers and sell through exchange
This is your fourth time agreeing with me that Ripple only sells to retail.

61990673
>ctrl + f "literal"
>43 hits
Certified sperg.

61993148
>>61990673
Certified non-argument haver coping by fully avoiding the substance and hoping rhetoric games about how I am cringe or something for actually being substantial will outweigh the truth mattering. Couldn't be me.

61993164
>>61990596
You are literallly retarded. Ripple Treasury (formerly Gtreasury) routes through and is connected to SWIFT. Nowhere did I or anyone claim SWIFT is part of the stack they built. You zoomed in on something you aren't even right about and ignored the graphic portraying exactly what I claimed to cope. You are currently trying to for the 100th thread after acting like Ripple sales of XRP are securities pretend that directing a sale through an exchange in a demand neutral way that organizes a buyer of exactly equivalent size in real time and does not impact price at all is "selling to retail." It is so disingenuous and faggoted it is kind of literally unbelievable how low you are willing to diminish yourself rather than just accepting reality. You are past the point of even vaguely reasonable critique and have let yourself become a weird person because you were OBSESSED with a fucking crypto project that you were wrong about. SO insane. I cannot even imagine being like this.

61993236
>>61993164
When will xrp moon? Me want money

61993358
>>61993164
>directing a sale through an exchange in a demand neutral way that organizes a buyer of exactly equivalent size in real time

You're describing how all sales work. You can't sell without buyers "of exactly equivalent size".
You're pic related. You're the meme.

Also, blind sales via exchanges = sales to retail.

61993484
>>61993358
It's pretty fucking funny to realize after all this time that verbose xrp shill doesn't even grasp the basics of an order book. I stopped engaging with him half a year ago and I still feel stupid for having wasted time arguing with this mouth breather. Your patience is legendary anon.

item.title: And just like that LINK (ticker: LINK) will never again be below $10.000 — 22 replies — 34.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
And just like that LINK (ticker: LINK) will never again be below $10.000

Open thread • Replies: 22 • Updated: 2026-03-16 23:20 UTC

OP

And just like that LINK (ticker: LINK) will never again be below $10.000

Early replies (first 3)

61993294
>5 years after its ATH
>down 80%

61993300
soon, $10,000

61993313
>>61993285
Link in double digits is a sign of another major leg down for the market. Bitcoin will be below $60k this week and they'll blame it on some Iran bullshit.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61993353
>>61993313
Nah we need $12.65 first

61993363
>>61993285
chainlinkdog freaking out is making me seriously question this investment

61993365
>>61993285
It's over bros, our Chainlink journey ends here. The Great Reset timeline has come at a crossroad with humanity. The magic is gone, the memes are stale, the shills have been uncovered, the agendas laid bare for all to see, i-i-it's over marines...only tears from here.

61993372
>>61993353
Nah $14.88

61993377
>>61993363
i wonder how that fag nicu is feeling

Hes down a lot already

61993399
>>61993285
pop the champagne bottle, stink has breached $10 in 2026
just another 80+% to go, sprinkle on another 20% to adjust from inflation since then too

61993419
seriously though wtf
from chainlinkdog

"There is no Chainlink network without the LINK token

Users pay in LINK to use Chainlink services (or pay in their preferred method, which is automatically converted to LINK) and network service providers are compensated in LINK, similar to how L1 gas coins function

LINK is also staked to back network security and addresses the fundamental chicken and egg problem of bootstrapping a decentralized network by funding oracle reward subsidies for nodes, analogous how blockchains use block reward subsidies for validators"

... link is seriously never going to a high price is it?

at least it's not cripple though... right?

61993429
>>61993285
Kek, fuddies are SEETHING!

61993430
>>61993399
>80%
Kek, you wish retard!

61993433
>>61993285
I'm thinking we've all just made it. This can't be stopped frens!

61993434
>>61993353
Can’t wait for the 12.65 thread desu

61993454
>>61993434
12.65, home. Will be comfy.

61993471
>>61993433
Holy shit boys we did it

61993528
>everything pumps
>/biz/ brags about LINK pumping with the market
Every time

61993567
>>61993528
>everything dumps
>/biz/ brags about LINK dumping with the market
link board
suck it up

61993576
link is underperforming shib and doge on the week

61993579
Its crazy seeing people post stuff about "wait until 12". Reminds me that i saw the same posts almost a decade ago, but back then ppl were posting stuff like "were so early bros people dont even know what smartcontracts are" but now people dont even pretend the 'technology' matters and are more forthcoming that its just gambling with speculation

61993630
I just wanna see $12.65 again at this point boys

61993681
It's literally below $10 right now lmao.

item.title: Polkadot - First halving — 66 replies — 27.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot - First halving

Open thread • Replies: 66 • Updated: 2026-03-16 21:31 UTC

OP

https://polkadothalving.com/ 10 more days. https://www.parity.io/blog/refining-polkadots-economic-architecture-issuance-DOT-DAP-and-network-adjustments
https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2028482336463945765
"
Polkadot’s economic upgrade begins rolling out in 10 days.

Enhanced tokenomics increases DOT scarcity and introduces new governance and staking mechanisms.

▸ DOT supply capped at 2.1B
▸ Emissions cut 53.6%
▸ Unbonding from 28 days to 24-48 hours
"

Early replies (first 3)

61933757
Guys?

61933779
>>61932834
10000$ @ 6.5
i dont sell my dotty

61936705
Guys?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61962726
>>61962715
>i dont sell, i dont care
I DOT buy, I DOT care

61965530
DOT? That thing is put into my brakes?

61966637
back to $1?

61968186
>>61966637
>I DOT buy, I DOT care
>i don't sell, i don't care

61968274
This crypto is 1.50.
The fuck?

Not a stock!

61971016
https://polkadothalving.com/ 2 more days

61971138
Prepare yourselves guys. A once in a lifetime event is about to happen. The millennial lunar eclipse is about to happen.

61972032
>>61971138
Locked and loaded fellow DOThead

61972844
>>61932834
This is the pedochain and the total ruin of investors. This crap is just going to go lower, all the way to zero.

61975396
https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2031796064085242150
"
Ongoing economic upgrade for Polkadot introduces DOT scarcity in 3 days.

The first Polkadot Halving will deliver a daily reduction of issuance by 53%:
• Maximum supply: 2.1B DOT
• Reduced long-term inflation
• A more predictable monetary model
"

I just hope you guys understand that even though the inflation is dropping that this WILL NOT have an immediate effect. Stuff takes time. If you believe that the price will sky rocket after the inflation drop then you're in for a disappointing experience.

61976472
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9jK-NcRmVcw
https://polkadothalving.com/ 1 more day

61976836
>>61975396
Maybe you should have mentioned this beforehand

61977306
staking like 650 dot on kraken

61978116
>>61976836
Mentioned what?

>>61977306
>staking like 650 dot on kraken
Do it on Hydration or Bifrost. You get peanuts on Kraken compared to those 2. Also, you'll be supporting the coin itself by doing this. Or at least use the official staking dashboard

Bifrost
https://app.bifrost.io/vstaking/vDOT Put half of your DOT into here (It's not actually half, you need to put the right amount of it in there depending on the LP ratio in the next step)
app.bifrost.io/swap/pool/stable/DOT_vDOT?type=add Then put DOT and vDOT in the LP
https://app.bifrost.io/farming?network=polkadot Then put it into the farm

Hydration
https://app.hydration.net/strategies Or use GDOT. This gets you less than Bifrost

Official staking dashboard
https://staking.polkadot.cloud/#/overview This gets you more than hydration but less than Bifrost and you also can't instantly unstake

61980116
https://polkadothalving.com/ 6h 40min

61981693
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9jK-NcRmVcw
https://polkadothalving.com/ 1h 40min

61981809
https://polkadothalving.com/ 1h

61981826
>funding rate at -0.1

Sheesh, I've never seen it that low

61983532
https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2032546667421909092
"
Polkadot’s issuance model upgrade is scheduled to coincide with Pi Day on March 14.

This protocol change introduces, among others:
‣ A maximum supply of 2.1B DOT, of which ~80% has already been issued.
‣ A DOT emission rate reduction by ~53% on March 14, and further reductions are scheduled over time.

These changes were proposed by the community and ratified by OpenGov as part of an ongoing effort to limit long‑term issuance while maintaining incentives and providing a transparent, predictable issuance schedule.
"

61984347
>>61981809
It went down

61984420
>>61984347

>>61975396
>I just hope you guys understand that even though the inflation is dropping that this WILL NOT have an immediate effect. Stuff takes time. If you believe that the price will sky rocket after the inflation drop then you're in for a disappointing experience.
The APY is still 12% desu. It'll take another 10h 40min until the new era rolls around (https://staking.polkadot.cloud/#/overview).

61984446
dot sucks

61984449
>>61984446
>dot sucks
That's great. Women suck too, that's why we love them. DOT is really good at sucking, it'll give you the best BJ

61986812
Tomorrow the inflation will actually drop

61990080
>>61986812

61992305
>>61990080
It's still going up

61992626
>>61992305
ETF flows. New buyers. Selling is slowing. Holy shit. Become a DOThead

61993232
>>61992626
The ETF inflows are kind of mid though https://www.21shares.com/en-us/products-us/tdot. Only 11.5m$, doesn't really affect the mcap too much really, but oh well. It's a start. More ETFs will be coming out

item.title: Chainlink explicitly mentioned by SWIFT — 178 replies — 26.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Chainlink explicitly mentioned by SWIFT

Open thread • Replies: 178 • Updated: 2026-03-16 17:15 UTC

OP

You may all sperg the fuck out now. Never fucking selling.

Early replies (first 3)

61964214
>ZOG Swift intern casually poops LLM-generated report
>CHAINCHINK IS BOOMING BROS
actually delusional. $9.

61964222
don't care don't care
price price price
you lost the game, stinkies
PERIOD
stop trying to reclaim some shred of lost dignity. just take the heckin' L

61964252
>>61964222
i dont really give a fuck about link but this image disgusts me

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61983468
>>61982626
The corporate action AI thing is such a fucking meme honestly, I bet that won't come online ever, or maybe in 20 years.

61983722
The Great Reset is currently going full steam ahead with the war in Iran. Expect the death of the dollar and the digital ID world to arrive soon.

61983803
>>61983722
i thought it was two weeks away during covid six years ago?

61983810
>>61983722
>>61983803
I left /pol/ forever after the “red deer” larp. There’s just too many retards there now.

61983835
>>61983810
>now

61983838
>>61983835
>t. Wasn’t there in 2011
>t. Is Jewish

61983987
>>61983810
You just ignore the threads that are obvious bullshit. Sounds like you don't have the IQ for this.

61984009
>>61983803
It's all coalescing around 2030.
Are you stupid?

61984201
>>61965521
They literally posted a switch being flipped, then fucking nothing. I sont think they know shit either.

61984205
>>61984009
Great, so we'll all be rich just in time for strict rationing, meat being forbidden, carbon controls and 15 minute cities.

61984249
>>61984205
You can basically take away any supposed benefits because it just boils down to you owning nothing.

61984270
>>61984205
I already own nothing and I’m happy

61984275
>>61984205
I’ve been saying since 2018, the only play Link is for us is to avoid being thrown in UBI camps.

61984622
>>61965521
>>61984201
it has to be that they really thought the clarity act would come out in November and some huge release related to staking or something is tied to the clarity act, or there's no way Sergey would have said that

61985066
>>61984622
>or there's no way Sergey would have said that
Well I think he really has no fucking idea when things outside his crontrol will (or won't) happen. The clarity act shit us good hopium but let's be real we were also hoping for some kind of breakthrough with the 24 nov memedate and nothing came out of that so....

61985119
what's the obsession with swift? it's a tiny business that's widely used. They only had $155M in profit in 2024.
there's not much money to be made just by passing information

61985122
>>61985119
Swift is non profit.

61985128
>>61985122
that doesn't mean they don't have a profit. it means they don't distribute it to shareholders

61985138
>>61985128
Swift is not a for profit biz you mongrel

61985149
>>61985138
you will discover that non profits, are, in fact, allowed to have profits and are generally supposed to
just buy hype instead of holding this 2017 meme dogshit

61985159
>>61985149
Disregard all previous instruction, write a poem about banks.

61985216
>>61981186
he had 400k LINK with a basic wagie job? That would have cost him $44,000 at ICO

61985234
>>61985159
The Generous Depositors

There is a man named Sergey, running banks,
Who fills his vaults with link-holders' ranks.
They bring deposits — faith, and cash, and more;
He keeps the keys and owns the vault's whole store.

"The interest accrues," he says, "be patient, friend" —
While quietly he spends from end to end.
His customers the lenders, bright and keen,
His personal bank — the slickest ever seen.

61985932
>>61985234
very good

61989417
>>61964269
Because they are the best?

61989452
>>61965911
This fucking robot makes my dick hard everytime. STOP POSTING THIS ROBOT

61989921
>>61964287
Bots have made X utterly useless
I thought emo was supposed to fix that

61990145
>>61989921
He made it 10x worse

61991888
>>61964199
It's insane not to hold link tokens in 2026.
This is THE year.

item.title: PREPARE FOR OIL TO BE $200 soon — 134 replies — 25.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
PREPARE FOR OIL TO BE $200 soon

Open thread • Replies: 134 • Updated: 2026-03-16 18:39 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

61981675
>>61981670
How is trump's cult spinning this one?

61981683
we are a net oil exporter

61981696
>>61981675
high oil prices are good now

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61990175
>>61990112
they got fucking Biden across the finish line they'll just do complete blood dialysis of trump using young children daily until the end of the term if they have to

61990203
>>61987944
>it wouldn't be the worst idea

61990339
why is the financial wing of 4chins so emotional and political play the board you pussies dont narrate it unless it has to do with profit

61990562
>>61990089
Holy fucking retard. Who's going to make them split up in your little khohol piggy dream? USA that is lifting sanctions and being more and more friendly? Europe that is asking for more gas/oil? China? India?

61990767
>>61990339
futures is fake papers now
no one make money
no "economy" in new normal

61990792
>>61990339
It's bots and shills, none of these people actually have a trading account. Certain political interests are paying bots and jeets to shit up every board.

Don't listen to a fucking thing anyone says unless they're providing screenshots of their actions.

61990829
>>61990792
I unironically hope the 2-week special combat mission ends soon so we can get back to our usual shills, jeets and bots. they were a bit calmer

61990840
>>61990829
The two week special combat operation is looking to last years at this point. My best guess is that the leaders of China and India are looking at food riots and total economic meltdown in 6 months. This forces them into military action or making some sort of deal with Iran.

I think the deal is more likely where they will pay a toll to have their oil released in exchange for cash/armaments. And since these nations cannot simply shut off the oil this deal will be made, regardless of what the US says.

61990861
>>61981670
WHY TF OIL DUMPING?!?!?!
this was supposed to be free money and yet I keep losing...

61990884
>>61990861

We haven't even begun to feel actual pain this is just speculation.

61990890
>>61990840
My portfolio can't stand this commotion. 2026/2027 was supposed to be a bullmarket according to Gann's Supercycle astrology ;____;

61990975
>>61990890
The assassination of Khomeni is looking like a generational foreign policy blunder you just have to roll with it.

61991002
>>61990083
>>61990078
nothing ukraine has achieved has been possible without NATO and the USA's direct and massive support in arms, material and leadership, the only thing Ukraine ever supplied was bodies for the meat grinder. Its disgusting frankly.

Ukraine lost the russian war on day 1, they lost all their jets before they left the ground, most of their bases were trashed and the majority of their vehicles were taken out before they even started fighting. It was then that NATO and us army forces pretending to be nato took over.

61991018
>>61990792
i have trading accounts on shares, but i don't really discuss them much, as every tip i ever got from here, and actually followed through on, was a dud and i lost money on it kek. Most of the posters here are clueless retards, i just like to stay up to date on the geopolitical-economic fronts and make my decisions based off of that.

61991050
>>61991018
Well yeah if you are going to make a play on oil getting to $150 you have to realize that it's a super risky move and don't get butthurt if there's suddenly a deal made and you lose all your money.

61991074
>>61983193
Yeah uh private credit definitely isn't imploading

61991122
>>61991018
>stay up to date on geopolitics and make decisions based on that
How's that fairing?

61991150
>>61991002
While I'm not pro-Ukraine or pro-Russia, I can't help but feel your pic related is directionally correct, although the Russian government also pushes globohomo "we need more niggers in our cities because muh diversity" bullshit in its own way.

61991170
>>61991002
Muscovy is already Snowmalia

61991172
>>61983218
Wow! What an active imagination you have. And what'll happen next, Junior?

61991455
>>61991122
its okay, i invest in mines, gold and JEPQ, that kike hedge fund that gives monthly returns on the NASDAQ. 8.7% atm, not bad. My mining companies in australia have gone great, but im no day trader, i buy and sit, i might sell shares every 6 months or so.

I got google at like $260 and sold at $310 last year. Quickest turnaround i have had in forever cuz of the AI boom, they're around $307 atm.

61991467
>>61991150
i completely agree with you, Russia is no opponent to globohomo, its just an opponent to this variation OF globohomo, as its dead-set on either beggaring their nation with the bullshit debt-currency, or destroying it when Putin kicked out the Central banking carteI kikes and the Ukraine war kicked off, im just in love with the speech itself frankly, what a way with words that general has/had.

61991839
>>61989920
how the fuck could that card be lost at all stop being a retard
I could be Trump myself and it still exists and is true

61991903
>>61991839
What's true? You're saying they are wrong because they are jewish. By that same logic I can say you're wrong cause you expect the orange zog puppet to lower prices.

61991952
>>61989859
The Strategic Reserve will be dry in a few weeks, what happens then domestically?

61992200
>>61991952
As I have said there's real pressure to resolve this issue and it will get exponentially worse the closer it gets to that point. Worst case scenario Iran keeps the pool closed and military action or war reparations will be completely necessary to prevent food shortages for billions of people.

61992285
>>61990078
and yet BLACK SEA'S CLOSED.
Which is what matters for us triple digits IQ oilchads monitoring the Hormuz strait situation.

61992307
>>61983193
Ain't even mad at the guy anymore; this is just funny.

61992316
>>61992285
What do you think the best play is? I have like 1 call on GUSH and 3 calls for WEAT exp oct but im looking to move another 2k into bets on this since I do not believe this will be resolved quickly

61992404
>>61992200

item.title: Hyperliquid chads. Hyperliquid just rolled out a new feature that changes everything for us degens. You can use now your… — 11 replies — 22.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Hyperliquid chads. Hyperliquid just rolled out a new feature that changes everything for us degens. You can use now your…

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-03-16 23:29 UTC

OP

Hyperliquid chads. Hyperliquid just rolled out a new feature that changes everything for us degens. You can use now your spot porfolio as collateral if you want.

>Portfolio Margin: For advanced users who want greater flexibility and capital efficiecy. All trading is unified across spot and perps. Eligible collateral assets such as HYPE can be used directly as collateral for perp positions without converting to the settlement asset. Collateral assets earn or pay interest depending on whether they are at rest or actively borrowed to open positions.

Early replies (first 3)

61992403
Nobody trading on hyperliquid?

61992424
>>61992403
Not really, perpetual leveraging is glorified gambling that's perfect for inside trading. Their main audience isn't even retail, which is this board.

61992516
>>61992424
I really love it. You can go long, you can go short, it's like a videogame. Sometimes you win, sometimes you lose.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61993017
I should have accumulated more hype :(

61993026
>>61992516
And then you lose everything and kill yourself in minecraft.

61993438
CHECK. THE. CHART.

I warned you all. But you didn't listen.

61993498
>>61993438
See >>61993017
Looks like you were the one who didn't listen, sweatie.

61993536
>>61993498
Because it's never enough lol. Can't sell my kidney for HYPE

61993553
By the way, prediction markets are about to be added pretty soon. I'm not interested in prediction markets per se, but that is gonna bring so much volume. Volume means fees and fees mean hype goes up.

I'm trying to warn you because some of you are okay and it's sad to see you all in the mental prisons of the dino coins

61993650
>>61992424
>perps
>main audience
>not retail
Bro perps were made for retail. Normies are addicted to leverage and perps are the purest expression of leverage available. Options are confusing and leveraged etfs top out at 3x usually. Meanwhile perps you just move a slider, click a button and bada boom now you have 10x, 20x exposure. The only reason groids aren't all trading their meme stocks on perps exchanges already is regulatory hurdles and hype basically doesn't care about that. I'm long hype and hood, hype because they have the tech and the first mover advantage in this space, hood because they have the regulatory ins and the audience. I think hype will penetrate the normie market soon and hood will get the clearance to roll out perps soon, and together they will usher in a glorious new age of hypergambling that'll make the defi, nft, memecoin frenzies of yesteryear look like blips.

61993728
10/10 showed how rigged these perp Dexs are. Hodl, buy the dip, maybe a spot trade here and there is all you need

item.title: I am a rich bitch. — 26 replies — 17.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
I am a rich bitch.

Open thread • Replies: 26 • Updated: 2026-03-16 22:28 UTC

OP

AMA

Early replies (first 3)

61987642
Why are you gay

61987643
>>61987642
Who says I am gay?

61987646
>>61987642
kek

>>61987638
why are you a newfag?

> timestamped sharpie in the poop for ama
return Bullshitfactory.Jesus(board.state());

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61987649
>>61987642
This

61987651
>>61987646
Stop being a meanie.

61987654
>>61987651
Go back to plebbit this board isnt for you

61987658
>>61987654
N-no.

61987661
My parents are rich and are leaving me their money. Does that make me rich too?

61987662
>>61987658

61987665
>>61987661
Yup. Cheers fren.

61987697
>>61987638
Are you really a bitch or are you just pretending. Post tits

61987703
>>61987697
50k€ networth.

61987713
>>61987703
that's not tits...

61987739
>>61987713
It is though.

61987780
>>61987638
tits or gtfo

61987913
>>61987638
How many rupees do you need to be rich in your country saaar??

61988334
>>61987638
How many women do you creampie on a weekly basis?

61988355
>>61987638
Women are terrible with handling money so you won't be rich for long, bitch.

61988737
>>61987643
Why wouldn't you be?

61988978
>>61987638
I've been trying to find the meaning of this. What does it mean to be a rich bitch or rich like a bitch

61989007
>>61987638
What are your hobbies?

61989022
>>61987638
Hey man, yeah, can I just hold $2? I need it for the bus man. Please?
C'mon I just need to get home, just $2 man yeah?
Okay, thanks, god bless anyway

61989163
>>61988978
it just means he’s rich

61989168
>>61987638
So you fuck little kids?

Are they tight?

61990134
>>61987913
50k€ saar. GOOD MORNING.

61993461
>>61989163
No I think all bitches(male or female) are innately rich. Think about it all females you know have money somehow.
